summary: I reviewed the technical design (`design.md`) against the 34 business requirements (`requirements.md`), grounding my checks in the discovery notes' facts about what the existing Define UI actually surfaces, and wrote a structured `needs_work` verdict to `verdict.json`. Tracing every requirement, I confirmed 32 of 34 are cleanly satisfied by the design's explicit R1–R34 traceability table and found essentially no over-design (the only unrequested elements — the `pause_requested_at` column and unpersisted `user:` params — are trivial, additive, and self-acknowledged). I raised two coverage gaps: **F1 (major)** — R19 and the restart-output assumption require a completed "Repeat from the Beginning" to show its fresh results inline, but the design only surfaces `discovery_notes` and `open_questions`, never the regenerated `business_requirements` (Define's headline deliverable, which discovery-notes F1 confirms has no UI surfacing today), so a person is asked to click Done without seeing the requirements they'd approve; and **F2 (minor)** — "Ask Human" is wired only as an anchor into the `questions.present?` block, so with no current open questions the button is a dead link and R14's free-form-notes path is unreachable, an empty-state the requirements critic explicitly deferred to design and which the design leaves unresolved.
